one_liner: PLTR +7.77% on a dual catalyst of an NVIDIA Nemotron AI partnership and the US Army's NGC2 command-and-control contract — government AI is expanding faster than the market expected.

price_action.vol_rank: Elevated — above 30-day avg, but not in top-5 sessions
price_action.intraday_story: PLTR opened at $120.28, pushed to a high of $128.25, then pulled back to close at $125.73 — a close below the intraday high that forms a mild bearish wick. The 1.35x volume is above average but notably below the heavy-volume days of April 9-10 (1.93x-2.32x). This is a newsflow-driven surge rather than a capitulation event, and the upper wick suggests some near-term overhead resistance around $128.

news_catalyst.headline: Palantir wins US Army NGC2 modernization contract; partners with NVIDIA to deploy Nemotron models in AIP
news_catalyst.summary:
Palantir landed two headline-level catalysts in quick succession. The US Army Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) contract designates Palantir's Foundry as the cloud data layer for the Army's highest-priority digital modernization program, moving from prototype to broader rollout — a government contract that signals multi-year recurring revenue. Simultaneously, the NVIDIA partnership embeds Nemotron open-source AI models across Palantir's AIP, Foundry, Ontology, and Apollo platforms for US government agencies, creating a secure air-gapped AI environment that no hyperscaler can easily replicate.

Bonus catalysts include Trump's financial disclosure revealing a significant personal stake in PLTR (raising the political tailwind narrative for defense contracts) and news that Michael Burry trimmed his short position. Together, these four items gave the market strong reasons to buy the stock. The Meta cloud announcement that hit most tech names this morning had zero bearing on Palantir — its government AI software is orthogonal to the compute-leasing business.

technical.summary:
PLTR has been in a consolidation pattern after the high-volume selloff of June 25 (1.51x, described as 'high-volume down day — distribution') which brought the stock from $112 area to $107. That down move was followed by recovery to $116 (June 29-30) with drying volume (1.09x → 0.91x) — consistent with the distribution pressure exhausting and buyers stepping back in.

Today's catalyst-driven surge to $128.25 breaks above the consolidation range and tests the June 25 prior high zone. The 1.35x volume is adequate to confirm the breakout but not so climactic as to signal exhaustion. Key watch: if PLTR can hold above $119-120 (today's open/low) on any pullback over the next 2-3 sessions, that level becomes a constructive LPS for a follow-on entry. The prior high to watch from Q1 is above $128 — the stock needs to digest this level.

The DCF at $184 base intrinsic value vs $125 price shows 31.7% upside — meaningful but not extreme given Palantir's growth premium. The valuation debate on PLTR has always been about whether government AI TAM justifies the multiple.

wyckoff_narrative:
The Wyckoff picture on PLTR is ambiguous — the engine labels it 'Consolidation / No Clear Phase' and no discrete VCP pattern has formed. The June 25 distribution-labeled day followed by the June 26 recovery could represent a Shake-Out (Spring) or simply sector noise. The stock has not built a clean range base.

What today's catalysts do is provide a potential Sign of Strength (SOS) impulse that bypasses the need for a clean base structure — major contract wins and institutional partnerships can kick off a markup phase without the full Wyckoff accumulation sequence. The risk is that without a proper base, the move lacks the 'weight' of absorbed supply and may be more vulnerable to giveback if broader sentiment turns. Watch the $119-120 level on any pullback as the line in the sand.

valuation.summary: DCF shows $184 base intrinsic value vs $125 current price — a 31.7% margin of safety. With Q1 2026 US revenue growth of 104% YoY and management raising FY2026 guidance to 71% growth, the DCF base may prove conservative. The risk is that the government AI premium could compress if budget cycles slow.

key_strengths: 104% US revenue growth YoY in Q1 2026
Army NGC2 and NVIDIA contracts create durable multi-year revenue
Government AI moat is difficult to replicate
key_concerns: Stock is already up significantly year-to-date before today's move
DOGE-driven government spending cuts remain a policy risk
fundamentals.summary: Fundamental API data unavailable. However, public earnings show 104% US revenue growth, raised FY2026 guidance, and expanding commercial + government footprint — the growth narrative is strong and today's news reinforces it.

risks: The intraday wick at $128.25 high vs $125.73 close shows sellers above $128 — the stock needs to consolidate before a clean breakout is confirmed
Government spending uncertainty: any DOGE budget-cut headlines targeting defense AI could reverse the contract-win tailwind quickly
If PLTR gives back below $114 (prior consolidation floor), the June 25 'distribution' reading was correct and the move is a bull trap
